Once price breaks the IB high or low theres a 98% chance it takes out the ETH (overnight) high/low in that direction...and an 80% chance it goes to prev. day hi/low, sweeping the book, before reversing back into range or traversing.

With all humbleness, There are not just 1 or 2 support and resistance levels. You have multiple ways to define S&R levels. In addition to this , you have weekly, monthly and  even yearly S&R levels. Some levels are Indicator based and some are pure price range based and the beauty is , you never know which S&R level will be respected & ignored . Some time , ppl fade at weekly level , another day ppl fade at yearly levels. That's why it's near impossible for retailers to be consistently profitable. This can also be called Randomness. one day 1+1 = 2 and another day the same 1+1=3 . Tats day trading for retailers.

Well done

At 2:56 you are not right.
 
Its not the level of the highs of the day 15th that's is interesting here, you look at the chart the wrong way. 
The morning session the 14th far left at 09:00 and 12:00 a double top with equal highs, is a strong area, with rejection from sellers. 
Note that top 2 doesn't reach the high fully, that's a strong short signal. 
Mark that area out as Previous day high, "resistance, double top, supply area, sell protection" or what ever your teaching call it. And keep it for the days to come.

At the 16th top: The break up that is denied by sellers around 3.375 ish , 16th at 1900 hrs, that is the real rejection from that area from the 14th.
That area is to be expected and marked up when making your daily bias before trading, already the 15th. 

So the 15th you speak about is true market makers move top influencing the 16th, but that area is not in play and bypassed by price making it invalid. Watch the top at the 14th, this is the ACCURATE priceaction level, and the real truth behind a trading opportunity in your exaple. You look at the wrong place.

The 14th top, shows you where you get in a short for minimum risk on a short on the 16th.

That is also the exit signal if you where in from the move up. 
This would have been a good move with obvious byers protecting the level by "support, supply and PO3", at the VERY low of the 16th, it just slightly dip and takes out the low of the 15th, and is a clear long signal. 

You dont need data flow to see executed orders in the market, just reading the candles gives you knowledge of what actual happens in real time. 
The reversal on Yesterdays low the 16th, can be the multible days previous low and the day before or even Weekly low. 
Learn to read the priceaction. The indicator just teach you to use a signal as an excuse for executing, not helping you to read and understand the market.
